1.text: I, Nephi, was born of good parents, so I was taught many things from my father's learning. I have been through many hardships in my life, but I have also been greatly blessed by the Lord throughout all my days. Having gained a deep understanding of God's goodness and mysteries, I am now writing this record of my life.
I am writing this record in my father's language, which combines the learning of the Jews with the Egyptian language.
I know that this record I am making is true. I am writing it with my own hand, and I am writing it according to my knowledge.

It was at the beginning of the first year of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah (my father, Lehi, had lived in Jerusalem all his life), and in that same year many prophets came forward, telling the people they must repent or the great city of Jerusalem would be destroyed.
So it happened that my father, Lehi, went out and prayed to the Lord with all his heart on behalf of his people.
As he prayed to the Lord, a pillar of fire appeared and rested on a rock in front of him. He saw and heard many things, and because of what he saw and heard, he shook and trembled greatly.
He returned to his house in Jerusalem and lay down on his bed, overcome by the Spirit and the things he had seen.

While he was overcome by the Spirit, he was carried away in a vision. He saw the heavens open, and he believed he saw God sitting on his throne, surrounded by countless angels singing and praising their God.
He saw One coming down from the middle of heaven, whose brightness was greater than the sun at midday.
He also saw twelve others following him, and their brightness was greater than the stars in the sky.
They came down and went across the earth. The first one came and stood before my father, gave him a book, and told him to read it.
As he read, he was filled with the Spirit of the Lord.

And he read, saying: "Sorrow, sorrow will come to Jerusalem, for I have seen your terrible sins!" My father read many things about Jerusalem — that it would be destroyed, and many of its people would die by the sword, and many would be taken captive to Babylon.

After my father had read and seen many great and marvelous things, he cried out to the Lord, saying things like: "Great and marvelous are your works, O Lord God Almighty! Your throne is high in the heavens, and your power, goodness, and mercy extend over all the people of the earth. Because you are merciful, you will not let those who come to you be destroyed!"
This is how my father praised his God, because his soul was filled with joy and his whole heart was overflowing because of the things the Lord had shown him.

Now I, Nephi, am not writing everything my father wrote, because he wrote many things he saw in visions and dreams, and he also wrote many things he prophesied and spoke to his children, which I will not include in full.

But I will write about my own experiences in my life. I am making a shortened version of my father's record on metal plates I made with my own hands. After I summarize my father's record, I will write about my own life.

I want you to know that after the Lord had shown my father, Lehi, so many marvelous things — especially about the destruction of Jerusalem — he went among the people and began to prophesy and tell them about the things he had seen and heard.

The Jews mocked him because of the things he testified about them, for he truly testified of their wickedness and their terrible sins. He testified that the things he saw, heard, and read in the book clearly pointed to the coming of a Messiah and the redemption of the world.

When the Jews heard these things, they were angry with him — just as they had been with the prophets before him, whom they had thrown out, stoned, and killed. They also wanted to take his life. But I, Nephi, will show you that the Lord's tender mercies are over all those he has chosen because of their faith, making them powerful enough to be delivered.

2.text: The Lord spoke to my father in a dream, saying: "You are blessed, Lehi, because of what you have done. Because you have been faithful and declared to this people the things I commanded you, they are now seeking to take your life."
The Lord commanded my father in a dream to take his family and leave into the wilderness.
My father was obedient to the word of the Lord and did as the Lord commanded him.

He left for the wilderness. He left behind his house, his land and inheritance, his gold, silver, and precious things. He took nothing with him except his family, provisions, and tents, and departed into the wilderness.

He traveled down toward the borders near the shore of the Red Sea, journeying through the wilderness along the area closer to the Red Sea. He traveled in the wilderness with his family, which included my mother, Sariah, and my older brothers, Laman, Lemuel, and Sam.
After traveling three days in the wilderness, he set up his tent in a valley beside a river.
He built an altar of stones, made an offering to the Lord, and gave thanks to the Lord our God.
He named the river Laman and it emptied into the Red Sea. The valley was on the borders near the mouth of the river.
When my father saw that the waters of the river flowed into the Red Sea, he spoke to Laman, saying: "I wish you could be like this river, continually flowing into the fountain of all righteousness!"
He also spoke to Lemuel: "I wish you could be like this valley, firm, steadfast, and immovable in keeping the commandments of the Lord!"

He said this because of Laman and Lemuel's stubbornness, for they complained about their father in many things because he was a man who had visions and had led them out of the land of Jerusalem. They said he had left behind their land of inheritance, their gold, their silver, and their precious things, only to die in the wilderness. They said he did this because of the foolish imaginations of his heart.
Laman and Lemuel, the oldest, complained against their father. They complained because they did not understand how God works — the God who had created them.
They also did not believe that Jerusalem, that great city, could be destroyed as the prophets had said. They were like the Jews in Jerusalem who tried to kill my father.

And it happened that my father spoke to them in the valley of Lemuel with power, being filled with the Spirit, until they shook before him. He silenced them, and they did not dare to say anything against him. So they did as he commanded them.
My father lived in a tent.

I, Nephi, was very young but I was large in body. I also had a great desire to know the mysteries of God, so I cried to the Lord. And the Lord visited me and softened my heart so that I believed all the words my father had spoken. Because of this, I did not rebel against him like my brothers did.
I spoke to Sam and told him the things the Lord had revealed to me by his Holy Spirit. And he believed my words.
But Laman and Lemuel would not listen to my words. Being grieved because of the hardness of their hearts, I cried to the Lord for them.
The Lord spoke to me, saying: "You are blessed, Nephi, because of your faith, for you have sought me diligently with a humble heart."
"As long as you keep my commandments, you will prosper and be led to a promised land — a land I have prepared for you, a land that is choice above all other lands."
"As long as your brothers rebel against you, they will be cut off from the Lord's presence."
"And if you keep my commandments, you will be made a ruler and a teacher over your brothers."
"For the day will come when your brothers rebel against me. I will curse them with a severe curse, and they will have no power over your descendants unless your descendants also rebel against me."
"And if your descendants rebel against me, your brothers' descendants will be a punishment to them, to stir them up to remember me."

3.text: I, Nephi, returned from speaking with the Lord to my father's tent.
He spoke to me, saying: "I have dreamed a dream in which the Lord has commanded me that you and your brothers must return to Jerusalem."
"Laban has a record of the Jews and also a genealogy of my ancestors, and they are engraved on plates of brass."
"So the Lord has commanded me that you and your brothers should go to Laban's house and get the records, and bring them down here into the wilderness."

My father said that my brothers complained, saying it was a hard thing he was asking of them. But I, Nephi, said to my father: "I will go and do the things which the Lord has commanded, for I know that the Lord does not give commandments to anyone without preparing a way for them to accomplish what he has commanded."
When my father heard these words, he was very glad, because he knew I had been blessed by the Lord.
I, Nephi, and my brothers traveled in the wilderness and went up to the land of Jerusalem.
When we arrived at the land of Jerusalem, I consulted with my brothers.
I said to them: "We should go to Laban's house and draw lots to see which one of us will go in to Laban and ask for the records the Lord has commanded us to obtain."
We cast lots, and the lot fell on Laman. Laman went in to Laban's house and talked with him while he sat in his house.
He asked Laban for the records engraved on the brass plates, which contained my father's genealogy.
Laban became angry and threw him out of his presence. He refused to let him have the records, saying: "You are a robber, and I will kill you!"
Laman fled from Laban's presence and told us what Laban had done.
My brothers became very discouraged and were about to return to our father in the wilderness.
But I said to them: "As the Lord lives and as we live, we will not go back to our father in the wilderness until we have accomplished the thing the Lord has commanded us."
"So let us be faithful in keeping the Lord's commandments. Let us go down to the land of our father's inheritance, because he left behind gold, silver, and many precious things."
"He did this because of the Lord's commandments, for he knew Jerusalem would be destroyed because of the people's wickedness."
"The time must come that they will be destroyed, including Laban. So let us go down to the land of our inheritance, and let us gather together the gold, silver, and precious things."
"And we will go to Laban and offer to exchange them for the records we want — the records the Lord has commanded us to obtain."
After I said these words, my brothers agreed, and we went down to the land of our inheritance. We gathered our gold, silver, and precious things.
We went up to Laban's house.
We asked Laban to give us the records engraved on the brass plates, in exchange for our gold, silver, and precious things.
When Laban saw how much we owned, he wanted it. He threw us out and sent his servants to kill us so he could take our property.
We ran from Laban's servants and were forced to leave behind our property, which fell into Laban's hands.
We fled into the wilderness. Laban's servants did not catch us, and we hid in a cave.

Laman was angry with me and also with our father. So was Lemuel, because he listened to Laman's words. Laman and Lemuel said many harsh things to us, their younger brothers, and they beat us with a rod.

While they were hitting us with a rod, an angel of the Lord appeared and stood before them, saying: "Why are you hitting your younger brother with a rod? Don't you know that the Lord has chosen him to be a ruler over you, because of your sins? Go up to Jerusalem again, and the Lord will deliver Laban into your hands."
After the angel had spoken to us, he left.

After the angel left, Laman and Lemuel began to complain again, saying: "How is it possible that the Lord will deliver Laban into our hands? He is a powerful man, and he commands fifty soldiers. He can even kill fifty, so why not us?"

I said to my brothers: "Let us go up again to Jerusalem. Let us be faithful in keeping the Lord's commandments, for he is more powerful than all the earth, and certainly more powerful than Laban and his fifty, or even his tens of thousands."

"So let us go up. Let us be strong like Moses, for he truly spoke to the waters of the Red Sea and they divided, and our ancestors came through on dry ground out of captivity, while the armies of Pharaoh followed and were drowned in the waters of the Red Sea."

4.text: I spoke to my brothers and persuaded them to go with me up to Jerusalem again.
We went up to Jerusalem. I had my brothers hide outside the walls, and I crept into the city and went toward Laban's house.
I was led by the Spirit, not knowing beforehand what I would need to do.
As I came near Laban's house, I saw a man lying in front of me on the ground. He had fallen down because he was drunk with wine.
When I came closer to him, I discovered it was Laban.
I saw his sword and drew it from its sheath. The handle was made of pure gold and was finely crafted. The blade was made of the finest steel.
The Spirit constrained me to kill Laban, but I said in my heart: "I have never shed the blood of anyone." I shrank back and wished I did not have to kill him.

The Spirit said to me again: "The Lord has delivered him into your hands." I also knew Laban had tried to kill us and that he had refused to listen to the Lord's commandments. He had also taken our property.
The Spirit said to me again: "Kill him, for the Lord has delivered him into your hands."
"The Lord kills the wicked to bring about his righteous purposes. It is better that one man should die than that a nation should dwindle and perish in unbelief."
When the Spirit had spoken these words, I still hesitated, thinking in my heart about what I had never done — shedding someone's blood.

The Spirit said to me again: "The Lord has delivered him into your hands, and I also knew that he had tried to take my father's life. He also would not listen to the Lord's commandments, and he had also taken our property."
The Spirit said to me again: "Kill him, for the Lord has delivered him into your hands."
So I, Nephi, obeyed the voice of the Spirit and took Laban by the hair of his head. I struck off his head with his own sword.
After I struck off his head with his own sword, I took Laban's clothes and put them on, every piece.
After putting on his clothes, I fastened his sword to my waist and went to Laban's treasury.
As I went toward Laban's treasury, I saw his servant who had the keys to the treasury. I commanded him in the voice of Laban to go with me to the treasury.
He thought I was his master, Laban, because he saw the clothes and also the sword at my waist.
He spoke to me about the elders of the Jews, knowing that his master, Laban, had been out among them that night.
I spoke to him as though I were Laban.
I told him I wanted to carry the engravings on the brass plates to my older brothers who were outside the walls.
I also told him to come with me.
He thought I was talking about the brethren of the church, because he believed I was truly his master, Laban. So he followed me.
He spoke to me many times about the elders of the Jews as I went toward my brothers outside the walls.

When Laman saw me, he was very frightened, and so were Lemuel and Sam. They were about to run from me because they thought I was Laban and that he had killed me and was now trying to take their lives too.
I called out to them, and they recognized my voice, so they stopped running.
When Laban's servant, Zoram, saw my brothers, he began to tremble and was about to run back to the city of Jerusalem.
I, being a large man, grabbed Zoram and held him to keep him from running away.
I told him that if he would come down into the wilderness to my father, he would have a place with us.
I spoke to him and made an oath that he did not need to be afraid — that he would be a free man like us if he would come down into the wilderness with us.
I also told him, swearing with an oath, that once we got to the wilderness we would not take his life.
When Zoram had made an oath to us, we stopped worrying about him.
We took the brass plates and Laban's servant and departed into the wilderness, heading toward my father's tent.
After we traveled through the wilderness to my father's tent, my parents were filled with joy. My mother, Sariah, had been extremely worried.

She had truly mourned because of us, for she thought we had died in the wilderness. She had also complained against my father, calling him a man of visions, saying: "You have led us out of the land of our inheritance, and my sons are no more, and we are going to die in the wilderness."
This is how my mother had complained against my father.
When we returned to my father's tent in the wilderness, their joy was complete, and my mother was comforted.

She said: "Now I know for certain that the Lord has commanded my husband to flee into the wilderness. I also know that the Lord has protected my sons and delivered them out of Laban's hands, and given them the power to accomplish the thing the Lord commanded them." This is how she spoke.

5.text: After we arrived in the wilderness to my father, he was filled with joy and also my mother, Sariah, was exceedingly glad because they truly had mourned because of us.
They were filled with joy and gave thanks to the God of Israel.
My father, Lehi, took the records engraved on the brass plates and searched them from the beginning.
He found that they contained the five books of Moses, which told about the creation of the world and also of Adam and Eve, who were our first parents.
He also found a record of the Jews from the beginning down to the start of the reign of Zedekiah, king of Judah.
And also the prophecies of the holy prophets from the beginning down to the start of the reign of Zedekiah, and also many prophecies spoken by the mouth of Jeremiah.

My father, Lehi, also found on the brass plates a genealogy of his ancestors. He learned that he was a descendant of Joseph — the same Joseph who was the son of Jacob, who was sold into Egypt and who was preserved by the hand of the Lord so that he could save his father, Jacob, and all his family from dying of famine.
They were also led out of captivity and out of the land of Egypt by that same God who had preserved them.
My father, Lehi, discovered the genealogy of his ancestors on the brass plates. Laban was also a descendant of Joseph, which is why Laban and his ancestors had kept the records.
After my father had read all these things, he was filled with the Spirit and began to prophesy about his descendants.
He prophesied that these brass plates would go to all nations, peoples, tongues, and people who were of his descendants.
He said that these brass plates would never be destroyed and would never again become dim with time. He prophesied many things about his descendants.
My father and I had searched the plates and learned that they were desirable — essential for preserving the commandments of the Lord for our children.
The Lord had wisely planned for us to carry them with us as we journeyed in the wilderness toward the promised land.

My father also found on the brass plates that he was a descendant of Joseph — the Joseph who was Jacob's son and who was sold into Egypt. This was the same Jacob who was the son of Isaac, who was the son of Abraham.
Now my father knew about the genealogy of his ancestors, and Laban was also a descendant of Joseph. For this reason, Laban and his ancestors had kept the records.
When my father saw all these things, he was filled with the Spirit and began to prophesy about his descendants.
He said these brass plates of the Lord's word would go forth to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people of his descendants.
These brass plates would never be destroyed and would never fade with time. He prophesied many things about his descendants.
Up to this point, my father and I had fulfilled the commandments that the Lord had given us.
We had obtained the records the Lord had commanded us to get and searched them, finding them to be very valuable, even absolutely necessary for preserving the Lord's commandments for our children.
The Lord's wisdom was in having us carry these records with us as we traveled into the wilderness toward the promised land.

6.text: I, Nephi, am not writing the genealogy of my ancestors in this part of my record, but I will include it later so it may be found on the plates I am making. For now, I will not include it here.
It is enough for me to say that we are descendants of Joseph.
It does not matter to me to write a full account of all the things of my father, because they cannot all be written on these plates since I need the space for writing about the things of God.
My purpose is to persuade all people to come to the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob and be saved.
So I am not writing things to please the world but to please God and those who are not of the world.
Therefore, I will command my descendants that they do not fill these plates with things that are not of worth to the children of men.

7.text: I want you to know that after my father, Lehi, had finished prophesying about his descendants, the Lord spoke to him again, saying that it was not right for him, Lehi, to take his family into the wilderness alone. His sons should take women as their wives so they could raise up children for the Lord in the promised land.
The Lord commanded him that I, Nephi, and my brothers should return to the land of Jerusalem and bring Ishmael and his family down into the wilderness.
I, Nephi, and my brothers again went into the wilderness to go up to the land of Jerusalem.
We went to Ishmael's house and gained favor in his eyes, so we spoke the Lord's words to him.
The Lord softened Ishmael's heart, and also the hearts of his household, so they traveled with us down into the wilderness to my father's tent.

As we journeyed in the wilderness, Laman and Lemuel, along with two of Ishmael's daughters and two of Ishmael's sons and their families, rebelled against us — against me, Nephi, Sam, Ishmael, his wife, and his three other daughters.
During this rebellion, they wanted to return to the land of Jerusalem.

I, Nephi, was grieved because of the hardness of their hearts. I spoke to them, to Laman and Lemuel, saying: "How is it that you are so hard in your hearts and so blind in your minds that you need your younger brother to speak to you — even to set an example for you?"
"How is it that you have not listened to the Lord's word?"
"How is it that you have forgotten that you saw an angel of the Lord?"
"How is it that you have forgotten the great things the Lord has done for us — delivering us from Laban's hands and even giving us the power to obtain the record?"
"How is it that you have forgotten that the Lord is able to do all things, according to his will, for the children of men, if they exercise faith in him? So let us be faithful to him."

"If we are faithful to him, we will obtain the promised land. And someday you will know that the word of the Lord about the destruction of Jerusalem will be fulfilled, because everything the Lord has spoken about its destruction must be fulfilled."

"For the Spirit of the Lord will soon stop trying to reach them, because they have rejected the prophets and thrown Jeremiah into prison. They have tried to take my father's life to the point that they drove him out of the land."

"If you will return to Jerusalem, you too will be destroyed with them. Now, if you choose this, go up to the land, and remember the words I have spoken to you — that if you go, you will also be destroyed. For the Spirit of the Lord compels me to speak this way."

After I, Nephi, had spoken these words to my brothers, they were angry with me. They grabbed me because they were angry and tied me up with cords, intending to leave me in the wilderness to be devoured by wild animals.
But I prayed to the Lord, saying: "O Lord, according to my faith in you, please deliver me from the hands of my brothers. Give me strength to break these bands that bind me."
After I said these words, the bands were loosened from my hands and feet, and I stood before my brothers and spoke to them again.

They were angry with me again and tried to grab me, but one of Ishmael's daughters, along with her mother and one of Ishmael's sons, pleaded with my brothers until they softened their hearts and stopped trying to kill me.
They became so sorrowful over their wickedness that they bowed down before me and begged me to forgive them for what they had done to me.
I freely forgave them everything they had done, and I urged them to pray to the Lord their God for forgiveness. And they did pray to him.
After they had finished praying to the Lord, we continued our journey toward my father's tent. We arrived at my father's tent.

8.text: After we had all gathered at my father's tent, my father, Lehi, told us that he had dreamed a dream — in other words, he had seen a vision.

He said: "I have seen a vision, and because of what I have seen, I have reason to rejoice in the Lord because of Nephi and Sam, for I have reason to believe they and many of their descendants will be saved."
"But because of what I have seen about Laman and Lemuel, I tremble exceedingly. In my dream, I fear they will be cast out of the Lord's presence."
"I dreamed I saw a dark and dreary wilderness."
"I saw a man dressed in a white robe, and he came and stood before me."
"He spoke to me and told me to follow him."
"As I followed him, I found myself in a dark and dreary wasteland."
"After traveling many hours in darkness, I began to pray to the Lord that he would have mercy on me, according to his abundant tender mercies."
"After I prayed to the Lord, I saw a large and spacious field."
"I saw a tree whose fruit was desirable and able to make a person happy."

"I went forward and ate the fruit, and I realized it was the sweetest of anything I had ever tasted — sweeter than anything I had ever tasted before. Its fruit was white, surpassing all the whiteness I had ever seen."
"As I ate the fruit, it filled my soul with the greatest joy. So I wanted my family to eat it too, because I knew it was the most desirable of all fruits."
"As I looked around, hoping to find my family, I saw a river of water. It ran near the tree whose fruit I was eating."

"I looked to see where the river came from, and I saw its source a short distance away. At its source, I saw your mother Sariah, Sam, and Nephi. They were standing as if they did not know where to go."
"I called to them and told them to come to me and eat the fruit, which was the most desirable of all things."
"They came to me and ate the fruit."
"I wanted Laman and Lemuel to come and eat the fruit too, so I looked toward the head of the river, hoping to see them."
"I saw them, but they refused to come to me and eat the fruit."
"I saw a rod of iron that extended along the bank of the river and led to the tree where I stood."
"I also saw a strait and narrow path that ran along the iron rod, all the way past the head of the fountain to a large and spacious field, like a world."
"I saw countless numbers of people, many of whom were pressing forward to get to the path that led to the tree."
"They came forward and started on the path leading to the tree."
"A mist of darkness arose — an exceedingly great mist of darkness — so that those who had started on the path lost their way, wandered off, and were lost."

"I saw others pressing forward, and they came and grabbed hold of the end of the iron rod. They pushed their way through the mist of darkness, clinging to the iron rod, until they came to the tree and ate its fruit."
"After they ate the fruit, they looked around as if they were ashamed."
"I looked around and saw a great and spacious building on the other side of the river of water. It seemed to stand in the air, high above the earth."

"It was filled with people — old and young, men and women — and their clothing was extremely fine. They were mocking and pointing their fingers at those who had come to the tree and were eating the fruit."
"After the people tasted the fruit, they were ashamed because of those who were mocking them, and they fell away onto forbidden paths and were lost."
"I also saw many others feeling their way toward the great and spacious building."
"Many of them drowned in the depths of the fountain. Many others wandered onto strange roads and were lost from my sight."
"Great multitudes entered that strange building. After entering, they pointed the finger of scorn at me and those eating the fruit. But we paid no attention to them."
"These are the words of my father: 'As many as listened to them fell away.'"
"Laman and Lemuel did not eat the fruit."

"After my father had spoken all the words of his dream or vision, which were many, he told us that because of the things he had seen in his vision, he feared greatly for Laman and Lemuel that they would be cast out of the Lord's presence."
"He was deeply concerned for them, and he urged them with all the feeling of a loving parent to listen to his words, that perhaps the Lord would be merciful to them and not cast them out."
"After he had preached to them and also prophesied to them of many things, he told them to keep the commandments of the Lord. And he stopped speaking to them."
"All these things my father saw, spoke, and prophesied to his family, along with other things, which are too numerous for me to write in this account."
"I have not written them all in this shorter account — neither could I write all the things my father wrote. I have written enough for my purposes."

9.text: All these things my father saw, spoke, and prophesied to his children. He also spoke about a record of the Jews that would be carried by his descendants, with reference to the brass plates.

He said the brass plates should never be destroyed but should be passed down from one generation to another, preserved by the hand of the Lord until they go to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people.
After my father, Lehi, had prophesied and spoken all these things to his family, the Lord also spoke to him, commanding him to continue his journey into the wilderness.

So my father left into the wilderness. He went down toward the borders near the Red Sea, and he traveled in the wilderness with his family, which consisted of my mother Sariah, my brothers Laman, Lemuel, Sam, and also Ishmael and his family.
The Lord commanded my father that he should write upon these plates a record of the ministry and the prophecies — the plainer and more precious parts.

On my other plates, I have written a more complete account of the history of my people. I call the first plates "the plates of Nephi" after my own name; and these plates I also call the plates of Nephi.

10.text: Now I, Nephi, continue to write on these plates about what I have experienced. I will write more of my father's words, and also more of my own prophecies.
After my father finished speaking about his dream and urging them to be diligent, he spoke to them about the Jews.

He said that after they — meaning Jerusalem and its people — were destroyed, the many who were carried away captive to Babylon would return in the Lord's own time to possess the land of Jerusalem again.
He said that six hundred years from the time my father left Jerusalem, a prophet would be raised up among the Jews — a Messiah, a Savior of the world.
He also spoke about the prophets, saying that a great number had testified of this Messiah — this Redeemer of the world he had spoken of.
All mankind were in a lost and fallen state and would be forever unless they relied on this Redeemer.
He also spoke about a prophet who would come before the Messiah to prepare the way of the Lord.

He said this prophet would go into the wilderness and cry out: "Prepare the way of the Lord and make his paths straight, for there stands one among you whom you do not know. He is more powerful than I, and I am not worthy to untie his sandal strap." My father spoke much about this.
My father said this prophet would baptize in Bethabara beyond the Jordan. He also said he would baptize with water and that he would baptize the Messiah with water.
After he had baptized the Messiah with water, he would recognize and testify that he had baptized the Lamb of God, who would take away the sins of the world.

After my father had spoken these words, he told my brothers about the gospel that would be preached among the Jews, and also about the Jews dwindling in unbelief. After they had killed the Messiah who would come — and after he had been killed, he would rise from the dead and would make himself known, by the Holy Spirit, to the Gentiles.

My father spoke much about the Gentiles and also about the house of Israel — that they could be compared to an olive tree whose branches would be broken off and scattered across the whole face of the earth.
He said we must all be led as one body to the promised land, to fulfill the Lord's word that we would be scattered across the whole face of the earth.

After the house of Israel was scattered, they would be gathered together again. In short, after the Gentiles had received the fullness of the gospel, the natural branches of the olive tree — the remnants of the house of Israel — would be grafted in, coming to know the true Messiah, their Lord and their Redeemer.

In this way, my father prophesied and spoke to my brothers, and he also said many more things that I am not writing in this book, because I have written as much as I felt was sufficient in my other book.
All the things I have written about happened while my father was living in a tent in the valley he called Lemuel.

After I, Nephi, had heard all the words of my father about the things he saw in a vision and the things he spoke by the power of the Holy Spirit — which power he received through faith in the Son of God (and the Son of God was the Messiah who was to come) — I, Nephi, wanted to see, hear, and know these things too, through the power of the Holy Spirit, which is a gift of God to all who diligently seek him, whether in past times or in the time he would show himself to the children of men.
For he is the same yesterday, today, and forever, and the way is prepared for all people from the foundation of the world, if they will repent and come to him.

Anyone who seeks diligently will find. The mysteries of God will be revealed to them by the power of the Holy Spirit, in these times just as in times past, and in times past just as in times to come. Therefore, the course of the Lord is one eternal round.
Remember, O man, that whatever you do to one person, you do against everyone, and against God.
For the Lord truly taught my father, Lehi, that if those people at Jerusalem did not repent, they would be destroyed.
If they were destroyed, it would be according to my father's prophecies. And my father prophesied many things to them.

11.text: After I desired to know the things my father had seen, and believing that the Lord was able to make them known to me, as I sat pondering in my heart, I was caught away by the Spirit of the Lord to an extremely high mountain — a mountain I had never seen before and upon which I had never set foot.
The Spirit said to me: "What do you desire?"
I said: "I desire to see the things my father saw."
The Spirit said to me: "Do you believe that your father saw the tree he has spoken of?"
I said: "Yes, you know that I believe all the words of my father."

When I said these words, the Spirit cried out with a loud voice, saying: "Hosanna to the Lord, the Most High God, for he is God over all the earth, even above all! And blessed are you, Nephi, because you believe in the Son of the Most High God. Therefore, you will see the things you have desired."

"And this will be given to you as a sign: after you see the tree that bore the fruit your father tasted, you will also see a man coming down from heaven, and you will witness him. After you have witnessed him, you will testify that it is the Son of God."

The Spirit said to me: "Look!" And I looked and saw a tree. It was like the tree my father had seen, and its beauty was far beyond anything — surpassing all beauty. Its whiteness exceeded the whiteness of freshly fallen snow.
After I had seen the tree, I said to the Spirit: "I see that you have shown me the tree that is more precious than all other things."
He said to me: "What do you desire?"

I said to him: "I want to know what it means" — I was speaking to him as one person speaks to another, for I saw he had the form of a man, yet I knew it was the Spirit of the Lord, and he spoke to me as one person speaks to another.
He said to me: "Look!" And I looked as if to look at him, but I no longer saw him, for he had gone from my presence.

As I looked, I saw the great city of Jerusalem and also other cities. I saw the city of Nazareth, and in the city of Nazareth I saw a virgin, and she was extremely beautiful and fair — above all other virgins.
I saw the heavens open, and an angel came down and stood before me, saying: "Nephi, what do you see?"
I said to him: "A virgin, most beautiful and fair above all other virgins."
He said to me: "Do you know the condescension of God?"
I said to him: "I know that God loves his children, but I do not know the meaning of all things."
He said to me: "The virgin you see is the mother of the Son of God, according to the flesh."
After he said these words, I saw that she was carried away in the Spirit. After she was carried away in the Spirit for a time, the angel spoke to me, saying: "Look!"
I looked and saw the virgin again, this time carrying a child in her arms.
The angel said to me: "Look and see the Lamb of God — the Son of the Eternal Father! Do you know the meaning of the tree your father saw?"
I answered him, saying: "Yes, it is the love of God, which spreads into the hearts of the children of men. Therefore, it is the most desirable of all things."
He spoke to me, saying: "Yes, and the most joyous to the soul."
After he said these words, he said to me: "Look!" And I looked and saw the Son of God going among the children of men. I saw many fall at his feet and worship him.

I saw that the iron rod my father had seen was the word of God, and it led to the fountain of living waters — or to the tree of life — which waters represent the love of God. And I also saw that the tree of life represented the love of God.
The angel said to me again: "Look and see the condescension of God!"

I looked and saw the Redeemer of the world, whom my father had spoken of. I also saw the prophet who would prepare the way before him. The Lamb of God went forward and was baptized by him. After he was baptized, I saw the heavens open and the Holy Spirit come down from heaven and rest upon him in the form of a dove.
I saw him going out among the people, ministering to them in power and great glory. The multitudes were gathered together to hear him, and I saw that they cast him out from among them.
I also saw twelve others following him. They were caught away in the Spirit from before my eyes, and I did not see them.

After they were caught away in the Spirit, the angel spoke to me, saying: "Look!" And I looked and saw the heavens open again, and I saw angels coming down to the children of men, and they ministered to them.

He spoke to me again, saying: "Look!" And I looked and saw the Lamb of God going among the children of men. I saw multitudes of people who were sick with all kinds of diseases, and who were afflicted with devils and unclean spirits. The angel spoke and showed all these things to me. And they were healed by the power of the Lamb of God, and the devils and unclean spirits were cast out.
The angel spoke to me again, saying: "Look!" And I looked and saw the Lamb of God — that he was taken by the people. And I saw him judged by the world.
"I saw him lifted up on the cross and killed for the sins of the world."
After he was killed, I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together to fight against the apostles of the Lamb, for that is what the twelve were called by the angel of the Lord.

The multitudes of the earth were gathered together, and I saw that they were in a large and spacious building, like the building my father saw. The angel of the Lord spoke to me again, saying: "This is the pride of the world, and it represents the pride of the world and the fall of it. And great will be the fall of it."
The angel of the Lord said to me: "Look and see the destruction of the world." And I saw wars and rumors of wars among all nations and peoples of the earth, and I saw the destruction of many nations.

12.text: The angel said to me: "Look and see your descendants, and also the descendants of your brothers." I looked and saw the land of promise. I saw multitudes of people — too many to count.
I saw wars and rumors of wars among them. I saw many generations pass away in wars and conflicts.
I saw many cities — more than I could count.

I saw a mist of darkness on the face of the promised land. I saw lightning, and I heard thunder, earthquakes, and all kinds of tumultuous noises. I saw the earth and rocks splitting apart. I saw mountains crumbling into pieces. I saw the plains of the earth broken up. I saw many cities sink down. I saw many cities burned with fire, and I saw many cities collapse because of the quaking of the earth.
After I saw these things, I saw a vapor of darkness pass from the face of the earth. And I saw multitudes who had not fallen because of the great and terrible judgments of the Lord.

I saw the heavens open, and the Lamb of God came down from heaven. He showed himself to them, and I also saw and testify that the Holy Spirit of God fell upon twelve others, and they were ordained and chosen by God.
The angel spoke to me, saying: "Look and see the descendants of your brothers."
I looked and saw the people from the descendants of my brothers fighting against the descendants of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
The angel said to me: "Look and see the descendants of your brothers and also the descendants of your brothers' descendants."
I looked and saw that the descendants of my brothers were fighting against my own descendants, and the angel said to me: "These descendants of your brothers will overpower your descendants."
I looked and saw the descendants of my brothers overpowering my descendants, and they went forth in multitudes across the face of the land.
I saw them gathered together in multitudes to battle, and I saw wars and rumors of wars among them. In wars and rumors of wars, I saw many generations pass away.
The angel said to me: "Look and see the dwindling of your descendants among your brothers' descendants."
I looked and saw that the descendants of my brothers had overcome my descendants, and they had spread across the face of the land.
I saw them gathered together in multitudes, and I saw wars among them. Over the course of many generations, I saw wars and rumors of wars.
The angel spoke to me, saying: "Look and see the fountain of filthy water your father saw — the river he spoke of — and its depths are the depths of hell."
"The mists of darkness are the temptations of the devil, which blind the eyes and harden the hearts of the children of men, leading them into broad roads so that they perish and are lost."

"The large and spacious building your father saw is the vain imaginations and pride of the children of men. A great and terrible gulf separates them — the gulf being the word of the justice of the Eternal God and of the Messiah who is the Lamb of God. The Holy Spirit testifies of the Father and the Son."
The angel said to me: "Look and see the condescension of God!"
I looked and saw, just as I had seen before, the Lamb of God being taken by the people. I also saw and testify that he was lifted up on the cross and killed for the sins of the world.
After he was lifted up on the cross, I saw the multitudes of the earth gathered together.
The angel said to me: "Look and see the descendants of your brothers."

I looked and saw the descendants of my brothers fighting against my descendants, and because of the pride of my brothers' descendants and the temptations of the devil, I saw my brothers' descendants overpower my people.

13.text: The angel spoke to me, saying: "Look!" And I looked and saw many nations and kingdoms.
The angel said to me: "What do you see?" I said: "I see many nations and kingdoms."
He said to me: "These are the nations and kingdoms of the Gentiles."
I saw among the nations of the Gentiles a great church forming.

The angel said to me: "Look and see the formation of a church that is most corrupt above all other churches. It kills the saints of God, tortures them, and binds them, yoking them with an iron yoke and bringing them into captivity."
I saw this great and corrupt church, and I saw the devil as its founder.
I also saw gold, silver, silks, scarlets, fine linen, and every kind of costly clothing, and I saw many prostitutes.
The angel spoke to me, saying: "The gold, silver, silks, scarlets, fine linen, costly clothing, and prostitutes are what this great and corrupt church desires."
"For the praise of the world, they destroy the saints of God and bring them into captivity."
I looked and saw many waters, and these waters divided the Gentiles from the descendants of my brothers.
The angel said to me: "The wrath of God is on the descendants of your brothers."

I looked and saw a man among the Gentiles who was separated from the descendants of my brothers by the many waters. I saw the Spirit of God come down and work upon the man, and he went out upon the many waters — even to the descendants of my brothers who were in the promised land.
I saw the Spirit of God working upon other Gentiles, and they went out of captivity upon the many waters.
I saw multitudes of Gentiles upon the promised land, and I saw the wrath of God on the descendants of my brothers, and they were scattered and struck down by the Gentiles.

I saw the Spirit of the Lord upon the Gentiles, and they prospered and obtained the land as their inheritance. I saw that they were white and very fair and beautiful, like my people before they were killed.
I, Nephi, saw that the Gentiles who had gone out of captivity humbled themselves before the Lord, and the power of the Lord was with them.
I saw that their mother Gentiles were gathered together upon the waters and also upon the land to battle against them.
I saw that the power of God was with the Gentiles and also that the wrath of God was upon all those gathered to fight against them.
I, Nephi, saw that the Gentiles who had come out of captivity were delivered by the power of God from the hands of all other nations.
I, Nephi, saw that they prospered in the land. I saw a book, and it was carried forth among them.
The angel said to me: "Do you know what the book means?"
I said to him: "I do not know."

He said: "It comes forth from the mouth of a Jew." And I, Nephi, saw it, and he said to me: "The book you see is a record of the Jews, which contains the covenants of the Lord that he has made with the house of Israel. It also contains many of the prophecies of the holy prophets. It is a record like the engravings on the brass plates, except not as extensive. Nevertheless, it contains the covenants of the Lord with the house of Israel and is therefore of great value to the Gentiles."

The angel of the Lord said to me: "You have seen that the book came from the mouth of a Jew. When it came from the mouth of a Jew, it contained the fullness of the gospel of the Lord, of whom the twelve apostles testify. They testify according to the truth that is in the Lamb of God."
"These things go forth from the Jews in purity to the Gentiles, according to the truth that is in God."

"After they go forth by the hand of the twelve apostles of the Lamb, from the Jews to the Gentiles, you see the formation of the great and corrupt church, which is the most corrupt of all churches. They have taken away from the gospel of the Lamb many parts that are plain and most precious, and also many covenants of the Lord they have taken away."
"They have done all this to pervert the right ways of the Lord, to blind the eyes and harden the hearts of the children of men."

"Because of this, after the book has gone through the hands of the great and corrupt church, you see that many plain and precious things have been taken out of the book, which is the book of the Lamb of God."

"After these plain and precious things were taken away, it goes to all the nations of the Gentiles. After it goes to all the nations of the Gentiles — and after it goes across the many waters you have seen with the Gentiles who came out of captivity — you see that because of the many plain and precious things that were taken out of the book, which were plain to the understanding of the children of men according to the clarity that is in the Lamb of God — because these things have been taken out of the gospel of the Lamb, many people stumble, and the devil has great power over them."

"Nevertheless, you see that the Gentiles who have gone out of captivity and been lifted up by the power of God above all other nations upon the face of the land — the land that is choice above all other lands, which is the land the Lord God has promised your father that his descendants would have for the land of their inheritance — I say that after all this, you see that the Lord God will not allow the Gentiles to utterly destroy the mixture of your descendants who are among your brothers."
"And he will not allow the Gentiles to destroy the descendants of your brothers."

"Neither will the Lord God allow the Gentiles to remain forever in that awful state of blindness that you see they are in because of the plain and most precious parts of the gospel of the Lamb that have been kept back by that corrupt church whose formation you have seen."
"Therefore, says the Lamb of God: 'I will be merciful to the Gentiles, visiting the remnant of the house of Israel in great judgment.'"

"The angel of the Lord told me, saying: 'After the Gentiles stumble greatly because of the most plain and precious parts of the gospel of the Lamb that have been kept back by that corrupt church — which is the mother of prostitutes, says the Lamb — I will be merciful to the Gentiles in that day, says the Lamb, by bringing to them by my own power much of my gospel, which will be plain and precious, says the Lamb.'"

"'For the Lamb of God says: I will manifest myself to your descendants, so that they will write many things that I will minister to them, which will be plain and precious. And after your descendants are destroyed and dwindle in unbelief, and also the descendants of your brothers, these things will be hidden and come forth to the Gentiles by the gift and power of the Lamb.'"
"'And in them will be written my gospel, says the Lamb, and my rock and my salvation.'"

"'And blessed are those who seek to bring forth my Zion in that day, for they will have the gift and the power of the Holy Spirit. If they endure to the end, they will be lifted up at the last day and will be saved in the everlasting kingdom of the Lamb. And how beautiful upon the mountains are those who publish peace — even the tidings of great joy.'"

I saw the remnant of the descendants of my brothers, and also the book of the Lamb of God which had come from the mouth of the Jew, going from the Gentiles to the remnant of the descendants of my brothers.

After it had come to them, I saw other books that came forth by the power of the Lamb from the Gentiles to them, to convince the Gentiles, the remnant of the descendants of my brothers, and also the Jews who were scattered across the whole face of the earth, that the records of the prophets and the twelve apostles of the Lamb are true.

The angel spoke to me, saying: "These last records, which you have seen among the Gentiles, will establish the truth of the first records — which are of the twelve apostles of the Lamb — and will make known the plain and precious things that have been taken away from them. They will make known to all kindreds, tongues, and people that the Lamb of God is the Son of the Eternal Father and the Savior of the world, and that all people must come to him or they cannot be saved."

"And they must come according to the words that will be established by the mouth of the Lamb. The words of the Lamb will be made known in the records of your descendants, as well as in the records of the twelve apostles of the Lamb. Therefore, the two will be established as one, for there is one God and one Shepherd over all the earth."

"And the time will come that he will manifest himself to all nations, both to the Jews and to the Gentiles. After he has manifested himself to the Jews and to the Gentiles, he will manifest himself to the Gentiles and also to the Jews, and the last shall be first and the first shall be last."

14.text: If the Gentiles listen to the Lamb of God on the day he manifests himself to them in word and also in power, and truly take away the stumbling blocks that stand before them, and do not harden their hearts against the Lamb of God, they will be numbered among the descendants of your father. They will be numbered among the house of Israel, and they will be a blessed people upon the promised land forever. They will no longer be brought down into captivity, and the house of Israel will no longer be confounded.

"The pit that was dug for them by that great and corrupt church, which was founded by the devil and his children so that he might lead the souls of men down to hell — that pit which was dug for the destruction of men will be filled by those who dug it, bringing their own utter destruction," says the Lamb of God. "Not the destruction of the soul, unless it is cast into that hell which has no end."
"For the eternal purposes of the Lord will roll on until all his promises are fulfilled."
The angel spoke to me about the last days, saying there would be only two churches: the church of the Lamb of God and the church of the devil.
"Whoever does not belong to the church of the Lamb of God belongs to that great church — the mother of abominations. She is the whore of all the earth."
I, Nephi, looked and saw the whore of all the earth. She sat upon many waters and had dominion over the whole earth, among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people.

I saw the church of the Lamb of God, and its numbers were few because of the wickedness and abominations of the whore who sat upon many waters. Nevertheless, I saw that the church of the Lamb — the saints of God — was also upon all the face of the earth, but their numbers were small because of the wickedness of the great whore I saw.
I saw that the great mother of abominations gathered multitudes together upon the face of all the earth, among all the nations of the Gentiles, to fight against the Lamb of God.

I, Nephi, saw the power of the Lamb of God descend upon the saints of the church of the Lamb and upon the covenant people of the Lord, who were scattered upon all the face of the earth. They were armed with righteousness and with the power of God in great glory.
I saw that the wrath of God was poured out upon the great and corrupt church, so that there were wars and rumors of wars among all the nations and kindreds of the earth.

As there began to be wars and rumors of wars among all the nations belonging to the mother of abominations, the angel spoke to me, saying: "The wrath of God is upon the mother of harlots. And see, you are seeing all these things."

"And when the day comes that the wrath of God is poured out upon the mother of harlots — which is the great and corrupt church of all the earth, whose founder is the devil — then on that day the work of the Father will begin, in preparing the way to fulfill his covenants that he has made with his people who are of the house of Israel."
The angel spoke to me, saying: "Look!" And I looked and saw a man dressed in a white robe.
The angel said to me: "This is one of the twelve apostles of the Lamb."
"He will see and write the remainder of these things — and also many things that have already been."
"He will also write about the end of the world."

"Therefore, the things he will write are righteous and true. They are written in the book you saw coming from the mouth of the Jew. At the time they came from the mouth of the Jew, or at the time the book came from the mouth of the Jew, the things written were plain, pure, and most precious, and easy to understand for all people."
"The things this apostle of the Lamb will write are many of the things you have seen. And you will also see the rest."
"But the things you will see from now on, you must not write, for the Lord God has ordained the apostle of the Lamb of God to write them."

"And there are also others who have been, to whom he has shown all things, and they have written them. They are sealed up to come forth in their purity, according to the truth in the Lamb, in the Lord's own due time, to the house of Israel."
"I, Nephi, heard and testify that the name of the apostle of the Lamb was John, according to the word of the angel."
"And I, Nephi, am forbidden to write the rest of the things I saw and heard. Therefore, the things I have written are sufficient for me, and I have written only a small part of the things I saw."
"I testify that I saw the things my father saw, and the angel of the Lord made them known to me."

"I have seen that the descendants of my brothers will fight against the descendants of my people, according to the word of the angel. Because of the pride of my brothers' descendants and the temptations of the devil, I saw that the descendants of my brothers overpowered my people."
"After I saw these things, I saw the vapor of darkness pass from the face of the earth. And I saw multitudes and they had not fallen because of the great and terrible judgments of the Lord."
"I saw the heavens open, and the Lamb of God came down from heaven and showed himself to them."
"I also saw and testify that the Holy Spirit of God fell upon others, and they were ordained by the power of God."
"I, Nephi, am forbidden to write more of these things. Therefore, the things I have written are sufficient for me."
"Now I end my speaking about the things I saw while I was carried away in the Spirit. Even if not all the things I saw have been written, the things I have written are true. And so it is. Amen."
"Now I, Nephi, want to speak somewhat about the things my father prophesied concerning Joseph, who was carried into Egypt."

15.text: After I, Nephi, had been carried away in the Spirit and seen all these things, I returned to my father's tent.
I saw my brothers arguing with each other about the things my father had told them.
He had told them many great things that were hard to understand unless a person asked the Lord. Since they were hard of heart, they did not look to the Lord as they should have.
I was grieved because of the hardness of their hearts and also because of the things I had seen, which I knew must happen because of mankind's great wickedness.
I was overcome because of my afflictions, for I thought my afflictions were greater than all others because of the destruction of my people, for I had seen their fall.
After I had been strengthened, I spoke to my brothers, wanting to know what they were arguing about.
They said: "We cannot understand the words our father has spoken about the natural branches of the olive tree and also about the Gentiles."
I asked them: "Have you asked the Lord?"
They said: "We have not, because the Lord does not make such things known to us."
I said to them: "Why is it that you do not keep the Lord's commandments? Why do you choose to perish because of the hardness of your hearts?"

"Don't you remember the things the Lord has said? — 'If you will not harden your hearts and ask me in faith, believing that you will receive, and diligently keep my commandments, these things will surely be made known to you.'"

"The house of Israel was compared to an olive tree by the Spirit of the Lord who was in our father. We are the branch that has been broken off from the house of Israel and have been led away. Won't we eventually be grafted in again — brought back to the true olive tree?"
"The natural branches of the olive tree — the house of Israel — will be grafted in, coming to know the true Messiah, their Lord and their Redeemer."
My brothers, this is how I spoke to them and explained to them that these things would come to pass.
I spoke to them about the restoration of the Jews in the last days.

I rehearsed to them the words of Isaiah, who spoke about the restoration of the Jews, or the house of Israel. After they were restored, they would no longer be confounded or scattered again. I spoke many words to my brothers that caused them to be calm and humble before the Lord.
I also spoke to them about the things my father had seen, and also about the things spoken to me by the Spirit while I was carried away in the Spirit.
I told them about the things I had seen in my vision — that the house of Israel would eventually be scattered across the whole face of the earth and also among all nations.
I told them that Gentile nations would be set up as a mighty nation in the promised land, and our descendants would be scattered by them.

After our descendants were scattered, the Lord God would proceed to do a marvelous work among the Gentiles, which would be of great worth to our descendants. Therefore, it was compared to them being nourished by the Gentiles and being carried in their arms and upon their shoulders.

It was also of worth to the Gentiles, not only to them but to all the house of Israel, by making known the covenants of the Father of heaven to Abraham, saying: "In your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed."

I told my brothers I wanted them to know that none of the house of Israel can be scattered without having the power of the Holy Spirit with them, because the Lord keeps his covenants that he has made with Abraham, saying: "In your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed."
My brother Laman said to me: "What does the tree our father saw mean?"
I said to him: "It represents the tree of life."
He said to me: "What does the iron rod our father saw mean, that led to the tree?"

I told him it was the word of God, and whoever listened to the word of God and held firmly to it would never perish. The temptations and fiery darts of the adversary could not overpower them, blinding them and leading them to destruction.
I urged them with all the energy of my soul and with every ability I had to listen to the word of God and remember to keep his commandments always, in all things.
They said to me: "What does the river of water our father saw mean?"
I said to them that the water my father saw was filthiness, and his mind was so focused on other things that he did not notice the filthiness of the water.
I told them it was an awful gulf that separated the wicked from the tree of life and also from the saints of God.
I told them it was a representation of that terrible hell the angel told me was prepared for the wicked.

I told them that our father also saw that the justice of God separated the wicked from the righteous, and its brightness was like the brightness of a flaming fire that goes up to God forever and ever and has no end.
They said to me: "Does this mean the torment of the body in the days of testing, or does it mean the final state of the soul after the death of the physical body, or is it about temporal things?"

I said to them it was a representation of both temporal and spiritual things. The day would come when they must be judged for their works — the works they did in their physical bodies during their days of testing.

"If they die in their wickedness, they must also be cast off in the spiritual things related to righteousness. Therefore, they must be brought to stand before God, to be judged by their works. If their works have been filthy, they must be filthy. If they are filthy, they cannot dwell in the kingdom of God. If they could, the kingdom of God would be filthy too."
"But the kingdom of God is not filthy, and nothing unclean can enter the kingdom of God. Therefore, there must be a place of filthiness prepared for that which is filthy."

16.text: After I finished speaking to my brothers, they said to me: "You have declared harsh things to us — more than we can bear."

I said to them that I knew I had spoken harsh things against the wicked, according to the truth, and I had justified the righteous and testified that they would be lifted up at the last day. The guilty take the truth to be hard, for it cuts them to the core.

Now, my brothers, if you were righteous and willing to listen to the truth and pay attention to it so you could walk uprightly before God, then you would not complain because of the truth and say: "You speak hard things against us."
I told my brothers that if they kept the commandments of the Lord, all would be well with them. My father's words would be fulfilled according to the way he prophesied.
I had many things to say to them, which I also wrote on my other plates, telling a fuller account of wars, conflicts, and destructions among my people.
I said enough to my brothers, and my father had also spoken many things to them that I cannot write on these plates. He also spoke about the brass plates and about the things I have written.

After I, Nephi, had spoken to my brothers and explained these things to them, my father also spoke many more things to them. He also spoke about the Lord's mercies in bringing us out of the land of Jerusalem.
He spoke about how the Lord was merciful to us by not allowing us to perish in the sea.
He also spoke about the people in the promised land, saying they were a prosperous people according to the Lord's grace.
The Lord had reserved the land for the descendants of Lehi and for all who would be led by the Lord's hand out of other countries. There would be no king upon this land to rule over the Gentiles.

He prophesied that this land would be fortified against all other nations, so that whoever possessed this land would be free from bondage and captivity and from all other nations under heaven, if they would serve the God of the land, who is Jesus Christ, as had been made known through the things he had written.
I, Nephi, was deeply interested in these words, having written many of them. But I have written only what I thought was sufficient.
Now I continue with the account of my proceedings and my reign and ministry. I will say enough of my father's writings, as they are sufficient for now.
The Lord spoke to my father that his sons should take daughters of Ishmael as their wives.
I, Nephi, took one of the daughters of Ishmael as my wife. My brothers also took daughters of Ishmael as their wives, and Zoram also took the eldest daughter of Ishmael as his wife.
In this way, my father had fulfilled all the commandments of the Lord that had been given to him, and I, Nephi, had also been greatly blessed by the Lord.
The voice of the Lord spoke to my father during the night and commanded him to continue his journey into the wilderness the next day.

The next morning, when my father went to the entrance of the tent, to his great astonishment he saw upon the ground a round ball of unusual workmanship. It was made of fine brass, and within it were two spindles. One pointed the way we should go in the wilderness.

We gathered together whatever things we needed to carry into the wilderness, and all our remaining provisions the Lord had given us. We took seeds of every kind so we could carry them into the wilderness.
My father took the ball and headed into the wilderness.
We traveled in a nearly south-southeast direction, and after many days of traveling in the wilderness, we pitched our tents so we could rest and get food for our families.

As we rested, I, Nephi, was concerned about providing food, so I took my bow, made of fine steel, and went into the wilderness to hunt for food for our families. After I had hunted for food, my bow broke. Since it was made of steel, my brothers became angry with me because I had lost my bow, and we had no way to get food.
We returned to our families without food, and because they were so tired from traveling, they suffered greatly from the lack of food.

Laman and Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael began to complain greatly because of their sufferings and afflictions in the wilderness. My father also began to murmur against the Lord his God. They were all extremely sorrowful, even to the point of murmuring against the Lord.

They were in the middle of the wilderness by the Red Sea, and it happened that I, Nephi, had been afflicted by my brothers because I had broken my bow. Since their own bows had lost their spring, it became extremely difficult to obtain food.
We could not get food.

I, Nephi, being greatly afflicted because of the loss of my bow and my brothers' and Ishmael's sons' bows losing their spring, and because of the difficulty of obtaining food — I spoke to my brothers at length because they had once again hardened their hearts, even to the point of complaining against the Lord their God.
I, Nephi, made a bow out of wood and an arrow from a straight stick. I armed myself with a bow and arrow, a sling, and stones. I asked my father: "Where should I go to get food?"
He asked the Lord, because they had humbled themselves because of my words. I had said many things to them with the energy of my soul.
The voice of the Lord came to my father and told him to look at the ball and read the words written on it.
When my father saw the things written on the ball, he trembled greatly, as did my brothers and the sons of Ishmael and their wives.
I, Nephi, saw that the pointers on the ball worked according to the faith, diligence, and attention we gave to them.

There was new writing on them that was easy to read and gave us understanding about the Lord's ways. It was written and changed from time to time, according to our faith and diligence. By this we see that the Lord can accomplish all things through small and simple means.
I went up to the top of the mountain, following the directions given on the ball.
I killed wild animals, which provided food for our families.
When I returned to our tents carrying the animals I had killed, they saw that I had obtained food and they were overjoyed. They humbled themselves before the Lord and gave thanks to him.
We continued our journey, traveling in nearly the same direction as before. After we had traveled for many days, we pitched our tents again so we could stay for a while.
Ishmael died and was buried in a place called Nahom.

The daughters of Ishmael mourned greatly because of the death of their father and because of their afflictions in the wilderness. They complained against my father because he had led them out of the land of Jerusalem, saying: "Our father is dead. We have wandered much in the wilderness, and we have suffered much affliction, hunger, thirst, and fatigue. After all these sufferings, we must die of hunger in the wilderness."

17.text: We did continue to follow the directions on the ball, which led us into the more fertile parts of the wilderness.
After traveling for many days in the wilderness, we pitched our tents for a while so we could rest and get food for our families.
I, Nephi, went into the wilderness to hunt food. And the Lord said to me: "I will make your food sweet so that you will not need to cook it."
"I will also be your light in the wilderness. I will prepare the way before you, if you keep my commandments. So you will know that it is by me that you are led."
"After you have kept my commandments, you will arrive in the promised land. And you will know that it is by me that you were led."
"In the promised land, I will bless you and your descendants. None will be set up over them in the promised land unless they are brought there by the Lord's hand."
"I will consecrate this land for your descendants and those who will be with them, as a land of their inheritance forever, if they keep my commandments."

The Lord said to me: "After your father and family have traveled in the wilderness for eight years, you will arrive at a land I will call Bountiful, because of its much fruit and wild honey. I have prepared all these things for your family."
"After you have arrived at the land called Bountiful, you will come to the sea."
"There I will command you to build a ship."
"I will show you how to build the ship so that you can cross the waters."
I, Nephi, said to the Lord: "Where should I go to find ore to melt so I can make tools to build the ship in the way you have commanded me?"
The Lord told me where to go to find ore.
I, Nephi, made a bellows out of animal skins to blow the fire, and after I had made the bellows, I struck two stones together to make fire.
Until this time, the Lord had not allowed us to make much fire as we traveled in the wilderness, for he said: "I will make your food sweet so you will not need to cook it."
"I will also be your light. If you keep my commandments, you will be led toward the promised land. And you will know that it is by me that you are led."
The Lord also said: "After you arrive in the promised land, you will know that I, the Lord, am God, and that I, the Lord, delivered you from destruction. I led you out of the land of Jerusalem."
I, Nephi, worked hard to blow the fire and the Lord showed me great things.

When my brothers saw that I was about to build a ship, they began to complain against me, saying: "Our brother is a fool, because he thinks he can build a ship. He also thinks he can cross these great waters."
My brothers were not willing to work because they did not believe I could build a ship. They also did not believe the Lord had instructed me.

I, Nephi, was deeply grieved because of the hardness of their hearts. When they saw that I was grieved, they were glad in their hearts, to the point that they rejoiced over me, saying: "We knew you could not build a ship, because we knew you did not have good judgment. You cannot accomplish such a great work."

"You are like our father, led away by the foolish imaginations of his heart. He has led us out of the land of Jerusalem, and we have wandered in the wilderness for these many years. Our women have worked hard while pregnant, and they have given birth in the wilderness and suffered everything except death. It would have been better for them to have died before they left Jerusalem than to have suffered these afflictions."
"We have suffered in the wilderness for all these many years."
"We could have enjoyed our possessions and the land of our inheritance. We could have been happy."

"We know that the people in the land of Jerusalem were a righteous people, for they kept the statutes and judgments of the Lord and all his commandments according to the law of Moses. Therefore, we know that they are a righteous people. Our father has judged them and has led us away because we listened to his words. Our brother is like him." In this way, my brothers complained about us.
I, Nephi, said to them: "Do you believe that our ancestors, the children of Israel, would have been led out of the hands of the Egyptians if they had not listened to the words of the Lord?"
"Do you think they would have been led out of bondage if the Lord had not commanded Moses to lead them out of bondage?"

"You know that the children of Israel were in bondage, and you know they were burdened with tasks that were hard to bear. Therefore, you know it was a good thing for them to be brought out of bondage."
"You also know that Moses was commanded by the Lord to do that great work, and you know that by his word the waters of the Red Sea were divided, and they passed through on dry ground."
"But you know that the Egyptians were drowned in the Red Sea — Pharaoh's armies."
"You also know that the Israelites were fed with manna in the wilderness."
"You also know that Moses, by his word, according to the power of God that was in him, struck the rock and water came forth so the children of Israel could quench their thirst."

"Despite being led by the Lord their God and their Redeemer going before them, leading them by day and giving them light by night, and doing all things for them that were necessary for man to receive, they hardened their hearts, blinded their minds, and rebelled against Moses and against the true and living God."
"According to his word, he destroyed them. According to his word, he led them. According to his word, he did all things for them. And nothing was done except by his word."
"After they had crossed the river Jordan, he made them mighty enough to drive out the people living in the land — to scatter them and destroy them."
"Do you think the people in that land who were driven out by our ancestors were righteous? I say to you: No."
"Do you think our ancestors were more righteous than them? I say to you: No."

"All people are treated equally before God. 'Those who are righteous are favored by God.' But the people of that land had rejected every word of God, and they were ripe in sin, and the fullness of God's wrath was upon them. The Lord cursed the land against them and blessed it for our ancestors — cursed it against them for their destruction and blessed it for our ancestors so they could have power over it."
"The Lord created the earth so it could be inhabited, and he has created his children so they could possess it."
"He raises up a righteous nation and destroys the wicked nations."
"He leads the righteous to precious lands and destroys the wicked, cursing the land for their sakes."
"He rules high in the heavens, for that is his throne, and this earth is his footstool."

"He loves those who will have him as their God. He loved our ancestors, and he made covenants with them — with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. He remembered the covenants he made, and that is why he brought them out of the land of Egypt."

"He straightened them out in the wilderness with his rod, for they hardened their hearts just as you have done. And the Lord straightened them out because of their sin. He sent fiery flying serpents among them, and after they were bitten, he prepared a way for them to be healed. All they had to do was look, yet because of the simpleness of the way — or because it was easy — many perished."
"They hardened their hearts from time to time, and they rebelled against Moses and also against God. Nevertheless, you know they were led by his matchless power into the promised land."

"Now, after all these things, the time has come that they have become wicked, nearly ripe for destruction. I do not know, but I suspect the time is at hand that they will be destroyed, for I know the day will surely come when they must be destroyed, except for a few who will be led into captivity."

"The Lord commanded my father to depart into the wilderness, and the Jews also sought to take his life. You have been trying to take his life too, which makes you murderers in your hearts and you are like them."

"You are quick to do evil but slow to remember the Lord your God. You have seen an angel, and he spoke to you. You have heard his voice from time to time, and he has spoken to you in a still small voice. But you were past feeling and could not feel his words. Therefore, he has spoken to you like the voice of thunder, which caused the earth to shake as if it would split apart."

"You also know that by the power of his almighty word he can cause the earth to pass away. You know that by his word he can make the rough places smooth and the smooth places broken up. Then why are you so hard in your hearts?"
