"My soul is torn with anguish because of you, and my heart is pained. I fear you will be cast off forever. I am full of the Spirit of God, to the point that my body has no strength."

"Now, when I had said these words, they were angry with me and wanted to throw me into the depths of the sea. As they came to grab me, I spoke to them, saying: 'In the name of the Almighty God, I command you not to touch me, for I am filled with the power of God, and my body will burn you like dried reeds. Whoever lays their hands on me will wither like a dried reed. Do not dare touch me, for God will strike you.'"
I, Nephi, told them they should no longer murmur against their father and they should not refuse to work with me, for God had commanded me to build a ship.
I said to them: "If God had commanded me to do all things, I could do them. If he told me to say to this water, 'Become earth,' it would become earth. And if I said it, it would be done."
"Now, if the Lord has such great power, and has performed so many miracles among the children of men, how is it that he cannot instruct me to build a ship?"

I, Nephi, said many things to my brothers, and they were confounded and could not argue against me. They did not dare lay their hands on me or touch me with their fingers for many days. They were afraid to touch me because the Spirit of God was so powerfully with me that they dared not, or they would wither before me.

18.text: The Lord commanded me to build a ship, and he showed me how. I did not build the ship according to the methods known to men. I built it in the manner the Lord showed me, so it was not built after the manner of men.
I, Nephi, went to the mountain often and prayed to the Lord often. Therefore, the Lord showed me great things.
After I had finished building the ship according to the word of the Lord, my brothers saw that it was good and that its workmanship was extremely fine. They humbled themselves again before the Lord.
The voice of the Lord came to my father, telling him to get up and go down into the ship.

The next morning, after we had prepared all things — much fruit, meat from the wilderness, plenty of honey, and provisions according to what the Lord had commanded us — we went down into the ship with all our loads and seeds, and whatever else we had brought with us, every one of us with our families. We went down into the ship and set out to sea, driven by the wind toward the promised land.

After we had been driven by the wind for many days, my brothers, the sons of Ishmael, and their wives began to make merry and sing and dance and speak rudely, acting as if they had forgotten by what power they had been brought there. They became extremely rude.

I, Nephi, was extremely afraid they would provoke the Lord our God's anger and he would strike them because of their wickedness, causing us to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea. So I, Nephi, began to speak to them seriously.
But they were angry with me, saying: "We will not have our younger brother rule over us."
Laman and Lemuel grabbed me and tied me up with cords, and they treated me very harshly. But the Lord allowed it so he could show his power and fulfill the words he had spoken.
After they had tied me up so I could not move, the compass the Lord had prepared stopped working.

So they did not know where to steer the ship, and a great storm arose — a great and terrible tempest. We were driven back on the waters for three days, and they became extremely frightened that they would drown in the sea. Even so, they did not untie me.
On the fourth day of being driven back, the tempest began to be extremely severe.

We were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea. After we had been driven back on the waters for four days, my brothers began to realize that God's judgments were upon them and that they would die unless they repented of their sins. So they came and untied the bands on my wrists. My wrists had become very swollen, and my ankles were also very swollen and sore.
Nevertheless, I looked to my God and praised him all day long. I did not murmur against the Lord because of my afflictions.

My father, Lehi, had said many things to Laman and Lemuel, and also to the sons of Ishmael. But they threatened anyone who spoke for me. My parents, being elderly and having suffered much grief because of their children, were brought down — sick in bed.

Because of their grief and great sorrow and the wickedness of my brothers, they were very close to being taken out of this life to meet their God. Their gray hairs were about to be laid down in the dust — they were near death and about to be buried in a watery grave.

Also, Jacob and Joseph, being young and needing much nourishment, were grieved because of their mother's afflictions. Neither my wife with her tears and prayers, nor my children could soften the hearts of my brothers enough to untie me.

Nothing except the power of God, which threatened them with destruction, could soften their hearts. Therefore, when they saw they were about to be swallowed up in the depths of the sea, they repented of what they had done and untied me.
After they untied me, I took the compass and it worked according to my desire. I prayed to the Lord, and after I prayed, the winds stopped and the storm ceased, and there was a great calm.
I, Nephi, guided the ship so that we sailed toward the promised land again.
After sailing for many days, we arrived at the promised land. We went ashore and pitched our tents, and we called it the promised land.
We began to till the earth and plant our seeds. We planted all the seeds we had brought from the land of Jerusalem. They grew abundantly, and we were greatly blessed.

As we traveled in the wilderness, we found that the promised land contained all kinds of animals — the cow, the ox, the donkey, the horse, the goat, and the wild goat, and all kinds of wild animals that were useful for man. We also found all kinds of ores, including gold, silver, and copper.

The Lord commanded me to make plates of ore so I could engrave the record of my people on them. On the plates I made, I engraved the record of my father, our journeys in the wilderness, and the prophecies of my father. I also engraved many of my own prophecies.

I did not know at the time I made the plates that I would be commanded by the Lord to make these other plates. Therefore, the record of my father, the genealogy of his ancestors, and the greater part of our proceedings in the wilderness are engraved on the first plates I mentioned. The things that happened before I made these plates are more particularly told on the first plates.

19.text: The Lord commanded me to make these plates for a special purpose — that I should engrave on them the things of my ministry and of my people. On the other plates I should engrave the account of the reign of my people, their wars, and destructions. Therefore, I engraved these plates and did so to fulfill the Lord's commandments.
I did not know at the time what purpose the Lord had for making these plates, other than a wise purpose he had, which I did not know.
But the Lord knows all things from the beginning, so he prepares a way to accomplish all his works among the children of men. For the Lord has all power to fulfill all his words. And so it is. Amen.

Now I, Nephi, am writing about the things of God on these plates. For I consider nothing more sacred than the things of God, and therefore I will not write anything on these plates except the things of God.

If I make a mistake, the people of old also made mistakes. Not that I want to excuse myself because of other people, but because of the weakness in me, which is according to the flesh, I would excuse myself.

The things some people consider of great worth, both for the body and the soul, others consider as nothing and trample them underfoot. They even trample the God of Israel underfoot. I say "trample underfoot," but I mean it in a different way — they set him as nothing and do not listen to his counsel.
He comes, according to the words of the angel, six hundred years from the time my father left Jerusalem.

The world, because of their sin, will judge him as a thing of no worth. Therefore, they will scourge him, and he will bear it. They will strike him, and he will bear it. They will spit on him, and he will bear it because of his loving kindness and his long-suffering toward the children of men.

The God of our fathers, who were led out of Egypt, out of bondage, and also were preserved in the wilderness by him — the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob — gives himself, according to the angel, as a man, into the hands of wicked men to be lifted up, according to the words of Zenock, and to be crucified, according to the words of Neum, and to be buried in a tomb, according to the words of Zenos, about the three days of darkness that would be a sign of his death given to those on the islands of the sea, but more especially to those of the house of Israel.

"For the God of nature suffers," the prophet Zenos said, and the earth shall groan, and the rocks shall be split, and many of the kings of the isles of the sea will be moved by the Spirit of God to say: "The God of nature suffers."

As for those in Jerusalem, the prophet said, they will be scourged by all people because they crucify the God of Israel and turn their hearts away, rejecting signs, wonders, and the power and glory of the God of Israel.

Because they turn their hearts away, the prophet said, the day will come that they will be struck in the eyes and have a deep blindness. They will wander among many nations, and the prophet said they will be hated among all nations.
Nevertheless, when that day comes, says the prophet, that they will no longer turn their hearts away from the Holy One of Israel, then he will remember the covenants he made with their ancestors.

"He will also remember the islands of the sea," says the prophet. "All the peoples of the house of Israel I will gather in, says the Lord, according to the words of the prophet Zenos, from the four corners of the earth."
"The whole earth will see the salvation of the Lord, says the prophet. Every nation, kindred, tongue, and people will be blessed."
I, Nephi, have taught my people these things, and I have taught them the prophecies of many of the ancient prophets and also what my father had prophesied.
I have also taught them the words of Moses, and I have told them these things so they would know about the Lord their Redeemer.

I spoke to them about the prophet Isaiah, so that I could more fully persuade them to believe in the Lord their Redeemer. Therefore, I read to them from the writings of Isaiah and applied all the scriptures to ourselves, that they might be useful and instructive to us.
I spoke many things to them, that they might know about the actions of the Lord in other lands, among the peoples of the past.
I read to them from the brass plates, which contained the record of Moses, and also of the prophets.
I read many things to them that were engraved on the brass plates so I could convince them that the Lord was their God.
I read many things to them about the things of the Lord that had been done in other lands and among other people.

I read many things to them from the book of Moses. But to more fully persuade them to believe in the Lord their Redeemer, I read to them the things written by the prophet Isaiah, for I applied all the scriptures to us so they would be useful to us.

Therefore, I spoke to them, saying: "Listen to the words of the prophet, you who are a remnant of the house of Israel, a branch that has been broken off. Listen to the words of the prophet that were written to all the house of Israel, and apply them to yourselves, that you may have hope as well as your brothers from whom you have been broken off. For the prophet has written in this way."

20.text: Listen and hear this, O house of Jacob, who are called by the name of Israel and have come from the waters of Judah, or from the waters of baptism, who swear by the name of the Lord and mention the God of Israel — but not in truth or in righteousness.
Nevertheless, they call themselves of the holy city, but they do not rely on the God of Israel, who is the Lord of Hosts — the Lord of Hosts is his name.
"I have declared the former things from the beginning. They went out of my mouth, and I showed them. I did it suddenly, and it came to pass."
"Because I knew that you are stubborn, and your neck is an iron sinew, and your forehead is brass—"
"I declared it to you from the beginning, before it happened. I showed it to you so you could not say, 'My idol has done this,' or 'My carved image or my metal image has commanded these things.'"
"You have seen and heard all this. Will you not acknowledge it? From this time forward I am showing you new things — hidden things you have not known."
"They are created now, not from the beginning, even before today you had not heard of them, so you could not say, 'I already knew about them.'"
"You did not hear it, you did not know it, and your ear was not opened from the beginning. I knew that you would deal very treacherously and were called a rebel from the womb."
"For my name's sake, I will delay my anger, and for my praise I will hold back from cutting you off."
"I have refined you, but not like silver. I have chosen you in the furnace of affliction."
"For my own sake — for my own sake — I will do it, for I will not allow my name to be polluted, and I will not give my glory to another."
"Listen to me, O Jacob, and Israel my called, for I am he. I am the first, and I am also the last."
"My hand has laid the foundation of the earth, and my right hand has stretched out the heavens. I call to them, and they stand up together."

"All of you, gather together and listen. Who among them has declared these things to them? The Lord has loved him. He will fulfill his word, which he has declared by them, and he will do his pleasure against Babylon, and his arm will be upon the Chaldeans."
"Also, says the Lord: I the Lord — I have spoken. I have called him to declare. I have brought him, and he will make his way prosperous."
"Come near to me. I have not spoken in secret. From the beginning, from the time it was declared, I was there. And now the Lord God and his Spirit have sent me."
The Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel, says this: "I have sent him. I am the Lord your God who teaches you to benefit, who leads you along the way you should go."
"If only you had listened to my commandments — then your peace would have been like a river, and your righteousness like the waves of the sea."
"Your descendants would have been like the sand, and the offspring of your body like its grains. Their name would not have been cut off or destroyed from before me."
"Leave Babylon! Flee from the Chaldeans! With a voice of singing, declare, tell this, send it out to the ends of the earth. Say: 'The Lord has redeemed his servant Jacob!'"
"They did not thirst when he led them through the deserts. He caused the waters to flow from the rock for them. He split the rock, and the waters gushed out."
"Even so, although he has done all this and greater things too, there is no peace, says the Lord, for the wicked."

21.text: "Listen, O islands, to me, and pay attention, you peoples from far away. The Lord has called me from the womb. From my mother's body he has made mention of my name."
"He has made my mouth like a sharp sword. In the shadow of his hand he has hidden me and made me a polished arrow. In his quiver he has hidden me."
"He said to me: 'You are my servant, O Israel, in whom I will be glorified.'"
"Then I said: 'I have labored in vain. I have spent my strength for nothing and for vanity. Yet surely my judgment is with the Lord, and my reward is with my God.'"

"And now, says the Lord — who formed me from the womb to be his servant, to bring Jacob back to him — though Israel is not gathered, yet I will be glorious in the eyes of the Lord, and my God will be my strength."

"He said: 'It is too small a thing for you to be my servant just to raise up the tribes of Jacob and to restore the preserved of Israel. I will also give you as a light to the Gentiles, that you may be my salvation to the ends of the earth.'"

"The Lord, the Redeemer of Israel and his Holy One, says this to him whom man despises, to him whom the nations hate, to the servant of rulers: 'Kings will see and stand up, and princes will also worship, because of the Lord who is faithful.'"

The Lord says this: "In an acceptable time I have heard you, O islands of the sea, and in a day of salvation I have helped you. I will preserve you and give you as a covenant for my people, to establish the earth, to cause them to inherit the desolate places."
"That you may say to the prisoners: 'Go forth!' To those who are in darkness: 'Show yourselves!' They will feed along the roads, and their pastures will be on all high places."
"They will not hunger nor thirst. Neither will the heat nor the sun beat on them, for he who has mercy on them will lead them, and by the springs of water he will guide them."
"I will make all my mountains a road, and my highways will be raised up."
"And then, O house of Israel — some will come from far away, and some from the north and from the west, and some from the land of Sinim."
"Sing, O heavens! Be joyful, O earth! Break into singing, O mountains! For the Lord has comforted his people and will have mercy on his afflicted."
"But Zion has said: 'The Lord has forsaken me, and my Lord has forgotten me.' But he will show that he has not."
"Can a woman forget her nursing child, that she should not have compassion on the son of her womb? Yes, they may forget, yet I will not forget you, O house of Israel."
"I have engraved you upon the palms of my hands. Your walls are continually before me."
"Your children will hurry against your destroyers, and those who made you desolate will leave."

"Lift up your eyes and look around. All of these gather themselves together, and they will come to you. As I live, says the Lord, you will surely clothe yourself with them all as with an ornament and wear them like a bride."
"As for your waste and desolate places and your destroyed land — you will now be too cramped for the inhabitants, and those who swallowed you up will be far away."
"The children born during the time of your bereavement will say in your ears: 'This place is too small for me. Give me room to live.'"
"Then you will say in your heart: 'Who bore me these? I had lost my children and was alone, exiled and wandering. Who raised these? I was left all alone. Where did these come from?'"

The Lord God says this: "I will lift up my hand to the Gentiles and set up my standard for the peoples. They will bring your sons in their arms, and your daughters will be carried on their shoulders."

"Kings will be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers. They will bow down to you with their faces to the earth and lick the dust of your feet. And you will know that I am the Lord, for those who wait for me will not be ashamed."
"Can the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captives of the righteous be delivered?"

"But the Lord says this: 'Even the captives of the mighty will be taken away, and the prey of the terrible will be delivered, for I will fight against those who fight against you, and I will save your children.'"

"I will feed those who oppress you with their own flesh, and they will be drunk with their own blood as with sweet wine. And all people will know that I, the Lord, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob."

22.text: Now I, Nephi, am teaching you about the things I have read in Isaiah. There are many things that Isaiah spoke of that may be hard for many of my people to understand, because they do not know about the way Jews prophesied. For I have not taught my people much about the customs of the Jews.
I have not taught my people much about the customs of the Jews because their works were works of darkness, and their doings were doings of abominations.
So I am writing to my people — to all who will one day receive the things I write — so they may know about the judgments of God, that they come upon all nations, according to the word he has spoken.

Therefore, listen, my people who are of the house of Israel, and pay attention to my words. Even though the prophecies of Isaiah are not plain to you, they are plain to all who are filled with the spirit of prophecy. But I am giving you a prophecy according to the spirit in me, so I will prophesy according to the plainness that has been with me since I left Jerusalem with my father. For my soul delights in plainness for my people, that they may learn.

I delight in the words of Isaiah, for I came from Jerusalem, and my eyes have seen the things of the Jews. And I know the Jews understand the things of the prophets, and there is no other people who understand the things spoken to the Jews as well as they do, except they are taught in the manner of the things of the Jews.
But I have not taught my people the manner of the Jews. But I am teaching them that the day will come when God will visit all the house of Israel.

He will reveal his words to them — powerful words against them — for the purpose of convincing them of the true Messiah who was rejected by them. He will convince them that it is no longer necessary for them to look forward for a Messiah to come, for there should not be any to come, except a false Messiah who would deceive the people. For there is only one Messiah spoken of by the prophets, and that Messiah is the one who will be rejected by the Jews.

According to the words of the prophets, the Messiah comes six hundred years from the time my father left Jerusalem. According to the words of the prophets and also the word of the angel of God, his name will be Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

Now I have spoken somewhat about the things of the Jews and the things I have written on these plates. The things I have written are the things of God. This is what I want my people to understand: the words of the prophets can be applied to us, the house of Israel, because we are of the house of Israel.

The things that can be applied to all the house of Israel should also be applied to us as well, for we are of the house of Israel. We have the prophecies and the revelations of our prophets, and we also have the prophecies of the prophets written on the brass plates, who prophesied about the return of all the house of Israel from their scattering.
After the whole house of Israel has been scattered and confounded, the Lord God will raise up a mighty nation among the Gentiles upon the face of this land. Our descendants will be scattered by them.

After our descendants are scattered, the Lord God will proceed to do a marvelous work among the Gentiles, which will be of great worth to our descendants. Therefore, it is compared to them being nourished by the Gentiles and being carried in their arms and upon their shoulders.

It will also be of worth to the Gentiles, and not only to the Gentiles but to all the house of Israel, by making known the covenants of the Father of heaven to Abraham, saying: "In your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed."
I want my people to know that all the kindreds of the earth cannot be blessed unless he makes his arm bare in the eyes of the nations.
Therefore, the Lord God will proceed to make his arm bare in the eyes of all the nations, in bringing about his covenants and his gospel to those who are of the house of Israel.
He will bring them out of obscurity and out of darkness, and they will know that the Lord is their Savior and their Redeemer, the Mighty One of Israel.
The great and corrupt church, which is the whore of all the earth, must tumble to the earth. Great must be its fall.
The wrath of God is not upon the people who are righteous. It is the wicked people who he destroys by fire.

The righteous need not fear, for they are the ones who will not be confounded. But it is the kingdom of the devil that will be built up among the children of men — the kingdom established among those who are in the flesh.

The time will speedily come when all churches built up for gain, all those built up to get power over others, all those built up to become popular in the eyes of the world, and all those who seek the lusts of the flesh and the things of the world and do all kinds of wickedness — they are the ones who need to fear and tremble. They are the ones who must be brought low in the dust. They are the ones who must be consumed as stubble. This is according to the words of the prophet.
The time will come quickly that the righteous must be led like calves of the stall, and the Holy One of Israel must reign in dominion, might, power, and great glory.

He gathers his children from the four quarters of the earth. He numbers his sheep, and they know him. There will be one fold and one shepherd, and he will feed his sheep. In him they will find pasture.

Because of the righteousness of his people, Satan has no power. Therefore, he cannot be loosed for many years, for he has no power over the hearts of the people because they dwell in righteousness, and the Holy One of Israel reigns.
Now I, Nephi, say to you that all these things must come to pass according to the flesh.
"The things written on the brass plates are true, and they testify that a person must be obedient to the commandments of God."

"Therefore, you need not suppose that I and my father are the only ones who have testified and taught these things. If you will be obedient to the commandments and endure to the end, you will be saved at the last day." And so it is. Amen.
Now I, Nephi, am going to stop my writing. I have written what is sufficient, except for a few more things I need to write, which I must write.
For the Lord has made it known to me that the things I have written are true, and they testify of the truth. And they are made plain and clear so that people can tell what is right and what is wrong.
God has spoken and I have heard. His word is truth, and he has made it known to me. And I am commanded that I should seal up these plates.
Therefore, I have spoken to you as I was commanded. I have told you the things that I have heard, and I have prophesied.
And now I make an end of speaking. These are the things I was commanded to write. And thus I make an end.
